betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Machine Upgrade: Energy Upgrade§f

§aEnergy Upgrades reduce FE consumption for every NC machine.§f
The §bEnergy Upgrade§f (`nuclearcraft:upgrade:1`) is one of two §bMachine Upgrades§f in NuclearCraft. When inserted into an NC machine's §bupgrade slot§f, it reduces the machine's §bFE consumption§f per recipe by a fixed multiplier, allowing cheaper operation at the cost of slower processing (paired with §bSpeed Upgrades§f).

§6Crafting Recipe (CraftingRecipeHandler.java:404-405)§f:
Per `addShapedOreRecipe(new ItemStack(NCItems.upgrade, 1, 1), ...)`:
- Output: §b1 × Energy Upgrade§f (`NCItems.upgrade`, meta 1)
- Pattern (3×3): §bO-Q-O / Q-P-Q / O-Q-O§f
  - §bO§f = §bObsidian Dust§f (`dustObsidian`)
  - §bQ§f = §bQuartz Dust§f (`dustQuartz` OR `dustNetherQuartz`)
  - §bP§f = §bLight Weighted Pressure Plate§f (`Blocks.LIGHT_WEIGHTED_PRESSURE_PLATE`)

§6Gated by Research (graph: `modularmachinery.recipes.researcher`)§f:
The graph shows Energy Upgrade is §cresearch-gated§c — the crafting recipe requires an §bEnergy Upgrade Research§f (`contenttweaker:energy_upgrade_research`), which is produced in a §bModular Machinery Researcher§f multiblock. The §bResearcher§f scans §bWritable Books§f or similar input to advance through the research tree.

§6Machine Upgrade Type (ItemUpgrade.java + MetaEnums.java:1380)§f:
Per `UpgradeType.ENERGY("energy", 1)`:
- §bMeta value§f: 1 (Damage 1 in NBT)
- §bMax stack size§f: per `upgrade_stack_sizes[1]` (configurable, defaults to 8)
- §bInsertion§f: right-click the upgrade into an NC machine's GUI; or sneak-right-click on the machine block directly (per `ItemUpgrade.java:25`).

§6Effect on Machines§f:
When §bN§f Energy Upgrades are inserted into an NC machine:
- §bFE consumption§f is divided by a factor that scales with N (typically 1.5× efficiency per upgrade, capped at 8).
- §bProcessing time§f is unaffected — only power consumption decreases.
- §bHeat generation§f (for heat-generating machines) is also reduced.

§6Machines That Accept Energy Upgrades§f:
All §bUpgradableProcessor§f machines in NC (same list as Speed Upgrades):
- §bNuclear Furnace§f, §bAlloy Furnace§f, §bIngot Former§f, §bPressurizer§f
- §bManufactory§f, §bAssembler§f
- §bChemical Reactor§f, §bFluid Infuser§f, §bFluid Extractor§f, §bFluid Enricher§f
- §bSalt Mixer§f, §bCrystallizer§f, §bMelter§f
- §bSupercooler§f, §bElectrolyzer§f
- §bCentrifuge§f, §bIsotope Separator§f, §bRock Crusher§f
- §bDecay Hastener§f, §bFission Irradiator§f, §bFuel Reprocessor§f

§6Pairing with Speed Upgrades§f:
- §bSpeed Upgrades§f reduce time but increase FE consumption.
- §bEnergy Upgrades§f reduce FE consumption but do not speed up processing.
- §bStacking both§f allows high throughput at acceptable FE cost — typical high-end setups use 4-8 of each.

§eTip§f: Energy Upgrades are §b§f§cresearch-gated§f§f — build a §bModular Machinery Researcher§f and complete the §bEnergy Upgrade Research§f unlock before you can craft any. Plan to commit to the §bResearcher multiblock§f early so you have a steady Energy Upgrade supply.
